title: carya alba
intro: Carya alba, also known as the white hickory or shagbark hickory, is a deciduous tree native to eastern North America. The bark of the Carya alba tree has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial effects, making it useful for treating various health issues. The health benefits of Carya alba include relief from skin irritations, wounds, and fever, as well as potential anti-cancer and antioxidant properties. Carya alba can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is also used in traditional herbal remedies to treat a range of health conditions.
